rules.title: Agent rule docs must be indexed
rules.snippet: Keep AGENTS and rule docs routed through a small index instead of broad rulebook loading.

rules.title: Domain cannot import infrastructure
rules.snippet: Keep infrastructure dependencies outside domain modules.

rules.title: Import boundary config requires tests
rules.snippet: Add tests for importBoundaryPolicies before relying on them.

rules.title: Public API surface is budgeted
rules.snippet: Reduce public exports or split the facade into stable owned APIs.

rules.title: Barrel/facade files require explicit profile
rules.snippet: Do not add barrel/facade exports unless profile policy explicitly permits them.

rules.title: Public facade can expose only stable API
rules.snippet: Remove internal, experimental, and unstable exports from public facades.
triggers: Public facade can expose only stable API

rules.title: Internal modules cannot leak through public types
rules.snippet: Do not export public signatures that reference internal modules or raw internal types.
triggers: Internal modules cannot leak through public types

rules.title: Package and crate ownership files are required
rules.snippet: Add OWNERS, CODEOWNERS, or an ownership README for packages and crates.
triggers: Package and crate ownership files are required

rules.title: Domain cannot import UI
rules.snippet: Keep UI dependencies outside domain modules.

rules.title: Domain cannot import database clients
rules.snippet: Pass persistence through ports/adapters instead of importing DB clients in domain.

rules.title: Domain cannot import HTTP clients or servers
rules.snippet: Keep HTTP transport code at boundaries and pass domain values inward.
triggers: Domain cannot import HTTP clients or servers

rules.title: Adapters cannot be imported by domain
rules.snippet: Adapters depend on domain; domain must not depend on adapters.
triggers: Adapters cannot be imported by domain

rules.title: Generated code cannot depend on domain internals
rules.snippet: Generated outputs may use public contracts, not domain/internal modules.
triggers: Generated code cannot depend on domain internals

rules.title: Production source cannot import test support
rules.snippet: Move test helpers under tests and keep production source independent.
triggers: Production source cannot import test support

rules.title: CLI/main depends on application boundary only
rules.snippet: Keep CLI/main imports routed through app/application boundary modules.
triggers: CLI/main depends on application boundary only

rules.title: Circular imports are forbidden
rules.snippet: Break circular module references by extracting an owner or port.

rules.title: Boundary modules require invariant documentation
rules.snippet: Add BOUNDARY-INVARIANT documentation to every boundary module.

rules.title: Boundary conversion functions return typed errors
rules.snippet: Return Result/Either/typed error shapes from boundary conversion functions.
triggers: Boundary conversion functions return typed errors

rules.title: Raw boundary input must be converted
rules.snippet: Convert raw input into domain types at the boundary before passing inward.
triggers: Raw boundary input must be converted

rules.title: Boundary modules cannot contain domain decisions
rules.snippet: Move domain decisions out of boundary/adapter files and into domain owners.
triggers: Boundary modules cannot contain domain decisions

rules.title: Domain modules cannot import boundary modules
rules.snippet: Invert the dependency: boundary/adapters may call domain, not the reverse.
triggers: Domain modules cannot import boundary modules

rules.title: Boundary modules require negative tests
rules.snippet: Add invalid-input tests for boundary decoders and converters.

rules.title: Boundary raw type count is budgeted
rules.snippet: Keep raw DTO/input type count small and convert to domain types quickly.
triggers: Boundary raw type count is budgeted

rules.title: Boundary glob additions require waiver
rules.snippet: Add an explicit waiver or owner note for new boundary glob expansions.

rules.title: Boundary utility filenames are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use domain-specific boundary file names instead of utils or helpers.

rules.title: Boundary DTOs cannot leak into domain signatures
rules.snippet: Return domain types from boundary conversion functions, not raw DTO/input shapes.
triggers: Boundary DTOs cannot leak into domain signatures

rules.title: Strict profiles must fail on errors
rules.snippet: Keep failOn including error; use warnings for advisory debt, not disabled hard failures.

rules.title: Config precedence must be explicit
rules.snippet: Declare profileName and schemaVersion so profile plus local config layering is unambiguous.

rules.title: Profile name must be known
rules.snippet: Use a known locked profile name such as strict or ocentra-enforcer.

rules.title: Config changes require policy self-check
rules.snippet: When config changes, run and record policy-integrity or rule-coverage validation.

rules.title: Immutable rules cannot be disabled
rules.snippet: Remove enabled:false from immutable rules or change the registry lock only through a reviewed rule change.

rules.title: Immutable rules cannot be downgraded
rules.snippet: Do not lower immutable error rules to warning or info; fix the code or file a narrow waiver where allowed.

rules.title: Unsafe code requires governed waiver
rules.snippet: Keep allowUnsafeCode false unless a narrow owner-approved waiver is present.

rules.title: Public re-export allow mode is forbidden in strict profiles
rules.snippet: Use facade-only or forbid; do not globally allow barrel/public re-export policy in strict mode.

rules.title: Build scripts and non-registry dependencies require waiver
rules.snippet: Keep allowBuildRs, allowGitDependencies, and allowPathDependencies false unless a narrow waiver exists.

rules.title: Boundary glob changes require owner note
rules.snippet: Add a narrow owner note when changing raw type, facade, runtime string, or import boundary globs.
triggers: Boundary glob changes require owner note

rules.title: Rule disable requires expiry
rules.snippet: Even overridable/advisory disables must carry waiverId, owner, issue, reason, scope, expires, and remediation.

rules.title: Unknown config keys are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove misspelled or unsupported config keys; schema drift must be explicit.

rules.title: CI must use npm ci
rules.snippet: Use npm ci in workflows; npm install is not deterministic enough for gates.

rules.title: CI must run SBOM check
rules.snippet: Run SBOM generation/checks in CI before release or merge readiness.

rules.title: Hard CI gates cannot continue on error
rules.snippet: Remove continue-on-error from hard Enforcer, test, lint, and policy jobs.

rules.title: CI must not hide failing hard gates
rules.snippet: Remove || true and other shell-level exit-code bypasses from hard gates.

rules.title: CI action versions must be pinned
rules.snippet: Pin workflow actions by major version at minimum; do not use main, master, or latest.

rules.title: CI workflows must declare least-privilege permissions
rules.snippet: Add an explicit permissions block, usually contents: read.

rules.title: CI must run on pull requests and main
rules.snippet: Trigger hard gates for pull_request and pushes to main.

rules.title: CI must cover Linux, Windows, and macOS
rules.snippet: Run cross-platform gates on ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, and macos-latest.

rules.title: CI workflow must match Enforcer adapter contract
rules.snippet: Run the local Enforcer parity gate from workflow templates instead of custom weaker gates.

rules.title: CI cannot call legacy weaker commands
rules.snippet: Call ocentra-enforcer or ci:local, not legacy rust-rules-only gates.

rules.title: Branch protection policy is required
rules.snippet: Document required branch protection and required checks.

rules.title: CI must run npm test
rules.snippet: Keep the full Node test suite in the required CI parity gate.

rules.title: Required checks must include Enforcer
rules.snippet: Branch protection docs must name the Enforcer local/CI gate as required.

rules.title: CI must run rule and policy tests
rules.snippet: Run the rule/policy tests in CI before accepting validator changes.

rules.title: CI must run multi-language tests
rules.snippet: Run multi-language Rust, TypeScript, Python, and common checks in CI.

rules.title: CI must run MCP tests
rules.snippet: Run MCP tests and smoke checks so CLI/MCP parity does not drift.

rules.title: CI must run Enforcer self-scan
rules.snippet: Run the Enforcer against itself in CI.

rules.title: CI must validate schemas
rules.snippet: Run registry/schema/policy integrity checks in CI.

rules.title: CI must run secret scan
rules.snippet: Run the common secret scanner in CI before publishing changes.

rules.title: CI must run dependency policy
rules.snippet: Run dependency policy checks in CI.

rules.title: Single-source contract values must not be copied
rules.snippet: Import or derive values from the owner contract instead of duplicating literals.

rules.title: Dependency security audit must pass
rules.snippet: Fix high npm audit findings or cargo audit advisories instead of suppressing them.

rules.title: External npm package licenses must match policy
rules.snippet: Use approved licenses or add a reviewed project policy exception.

rules.title: Public API documentation is recommended
rules.snippet: Add a short rustdoc/JSDoc/docstring for exported or public API, or disable/downgrade this advisory in project policy.

rules.title: Rule docs must include required teaching sections
rules.snippet: Add Covered Rules, Fails, Passes, Fix Recipe, and Validator sections to routed rule docs.

rules.title: Review and proof rules must name proof evidence
rules.snippet: Add a proof, checklist, or review evidence section for review/proof-oriented rules.

rules.title: Source rule docs must include fail and pass code blocks
rules.snippet: Add one bad and one good fenced code example for source-level rule docs.

rules.title: Tagged rule doc code blocks must stay parseable
rules.snippet: Fix malformed fenced JSON or unbalanced tagged examples before shipping docs.

rules.title: Fix snippets must stay compact
rules.snippet: Keep registry fix snippets at or below 240 characters.

rules.title: Registry doc anchors must be stable lowercase anchors
rules.snippet: Use lowercase markdown anchors generated from headings; do not invent mixed-case or unstable anchors.

rules.title: Immutable rule docs must use mandatory language
rules.snippet: Use must, forbidden, or required instead of should for immutable rules.

rules.title: Docs must not make legacy aliases canonical
rules.snippet: Document rust-rules as a compatibility alias, not the canonical product name.

rules.title: Docs cannot describe the pack as Rust-only
rules.snippet: Update stale Rust-only wording when TS, Python, or common rules are registered.

rules.title: Advisory rule docs must explain promotion
rules.snippet: Document how a profile can promote advisory warnings to errors.

rules.title: Rule docs and registry must stay in sync
rules.snippet: Add a registry entry for each routed rule doc ID or remove the stale doc reference.

rules.title: Human output must be deterministic
rules.snippet: Sort human-readable findings by normalized file, line, and rule ID.

rules.title: Validators must not use undeclared network access
rules.snippet: Keep scanner/checker code offline unless the rule explicitly declares a network capability.

rules.title: Validator source must be self-scanned
rules.snippet: Run Enforcer self-scan in CI over scripts, src, mcp, rules, and schemas.

rules.title: Enforcer source cannot carry temporary bypasses
rules.snippet: Remove TODO, FIXME, HACK, temporary, and bypass markers from policy-critical source.

rules.title: Generated JSON schemas must match Effect schemas
rules.snippet: Regenerate schema artifacts when Effect schema contracts change.

rules.title: CLI and MCP behavior must match
rules.snippet: Keep CLI and MCP route, explain, scan, check, and coordination behavior in parity tests.

rules.title: Registry docs must point to stable anchors
rules.snippet: Point each registry rule at an existing routed doc anchor such as rules/common/policy.md#covered-rules.

rules.title: Scanner-emitted rule IDs must be registered
rules.snippet: Register every emitted ruleId in rules/rules.json before shipping the scanner.

rules.title: Enforced rules must have fixture evidence
rules.snippet: Add fail and pass fixture evidence for validator-backed rules or explicitly mark review-only rules.

rules.title: Rule IDs must be locked
rules.snippet: Update rules/rule-id-lock.json intentionally when adding rules; never remove or renumber existing IDs silently.

rules.title: Rule IDs must be unique
rules.snippet: Keep exactly one registry row per ruleId.

rules.title: Rule metadata must not drift
rules.snippet: Keep title and snippet in rules/rules.json aligned with validator metadata or remove duplicate metadata sources.

rules.title: Violation reports must be complete
rules.snippet: Every finding must include ruleId, title, file, line, detail, doc, snippet, and source.

rules.title: JSON output must be deterministic
rules.snippet: Sort output and avoid volatile fields in scanner/check JSON reports.

rules.title: Policy-critical mutations require stronger proof
rules.snippet: Run rule coverage, policy integrity, fixture tests, MCP parity, self-scan, and CI governance before accepting policy-critical edits.

rules.title: Generated artifacts must not be committed as source
rules.snippet: Generate artifacts in CI/build output, not in tracked source folders.

rules.title: Generated output folders must not be committed as source
rules.snippet: Keep proof output, test results, reports, and generated build artifacts in ignored output folders or CI artifacts.

rules.title: Generated directories require ignore policy
rules.snippet: Keep generated outputs ignored unless the profile explicitly tracks them.

rules.title: Generated snapshots must be stable
rules.snippet: Redact timestamps, random IDs, and machine-local paths from generated snapshots.

rules.title: Generated files require source owner provenance
rules.snippet: Stamp generated files with generator and source-owner provenance.
triggers: Generated files require source owner provenance
Generated files cannot be edited manually
rules.title: Generated files cannot be edited manually
rules.snippet: Regenerate artifacts from their source owner instead of hand-editing output.

rules.title: Generated contract artifacts require source hash
rules.snippet: Regenerate contract artifacts with a source schema or contract hash.
triggers: Generated contract artifacts require source hash

rules.title: Generated schema files must be reproducible
rules.snippet: Stamp generated schemas with deterministic generator and schema hash metadata.
triggers: Generated schema files must be reproducible
Runtime output directories cannot be tracked
rules.title: Runtime output directories cannot be tracked
rules.snippet: Keep coverage, playwright-report, test-results, and output directories out of source.

rules.title: Generated files cannot be single source of truth
rules.snippet: Move ownership to source schemas/templates and treat generated files as outputs.
triggers: Generated files cannot be single source of truth

rules.title: Generated code cannot contain suppressions
rules.snippet: Fix the generator or generated source policy instead of emitting bypass comments.

triggers: Generated code cannot live in domain modules
rules.title: Generated code cannot live in domain modules
rules.snippet: Move generated artifacts under configured generated/boundary folders.

rules.title: Harnessed command failed
rules.snippet: Read compact diagnostics first, then inspect bounded raw artifacts only if needed.

rules.title: Harness runs must identify command lifecycle
rules.snippet: Keep runId, command, cwd, startedAt, endedAt, and exitCode in every harness summary.

rules.title: Harness artifacts cannot escape storage
rules.snippet: Reject artifact paths that resolve outside the target repository storage root.

rules.title: Pinned proof runs must survive pruning
rules.snippet: Keep pinned PR-ready/proof runs even when normal retention would prune old runs.

rules.title: Failed harness commands must fail process gates
rules.snippet: Return ok false and non-zero CLI status when a command or guard fails.

rules.title: Harness JSON output must have schema artifacts
rules.snippet: Keep run report and diagnostic JSON schemas generated for MCP and non-Effect consumers.

rules.title: Harness human output must redact secrets
rules.snippet: Redact tokens, private keys, and credentials before exposing compact or raw artifacts.

rules.title: Harness commands must avoid shell by default
rules.snippet: Run commands through argument arrays with shell disabled unless explicitly requested by policy.

rules.title: Raw harness logs must be bounded and redacted
rules.snippet: Bound artifact reads and redact secrets before logs or artifacts are returned.

rules.title: Harness diagnostics must be sorted deterministically
rules.snippet: Sort diagnostics by normalized file, line, rule ID, and message.

rules.title: Harness parsers must not throw on malformed output
rules.snippet: Emit a parser diagnostic instead of crashing on malformed tool output.

rules.title: Cargo JSON diagnostics must normalize
rules.snippet: Normalize rustc/cargo compiler-message JSON into rule, file, line, severity diagnostics.

rules.title: ESLint JSON diagnostics must normalize
rules.snippet: Normalize ESLint JSON filePath/messages output into compact diagnostics.

rules.title: Python tool diagnostics must normalize
rules.snippet: Normalize Ruff, Pyright, mypy-style, and pytest diagnostics into compact records.

rules.title: SARIF diagnostics must normalize
rules.snippet: Normalize SARIF runs/results into file, line, severity, rule ID diagnostics.

rules.title: Last failure must avoid raw terminal dumps
rules.snippet: Expose compact failed-run diagnostics through last-failure queries.

rules.title: MCP must expose CLI scan and check equivalents
rules.snippet: Keep route, scan, check, doctor, explain, run, proof, and coordination tools available over MCP.

rules.title: MCP scan must not mutate target repositories
rules.snippet: Run scan/check/explain/route paths as read-only operations unless the tool is explicitly write-capable.

rules.title: MCP write tools cannot be generic action dispatchers
rules.snippet: Use dedicated claim/release/message tools or reject mismatched action fields.

rules.title: MCP errors must be structured JSON
rules.snippet: Return JSON error objects instead of raw text or stack dumps.

rules.title: MCP inputs must be schema decoded
rules.snippet: Decode every external MCP input through Effect Schema or equivalent strict schemas.

rules.title: MCP must reject unknown tool arguments
rules.snippet: Use additionalProperties:false and decoder checks so unknown MCP args do not get ignored.

rules.title: MCP summary output must be bounded
rules.snippet: Respect summaryOnly/includeScope controls for compact MCP responses.

rules.title: MCP diagnostic limits must be enforced
rules.snippet: Apply diagnosticLimit before returning findings to Codex.

rules.title: Stale MCP write processes must fail closed
rules.snippet: Refuse coordination writes when the loaded MCP server fingerprint is stale.

rules.title: MCP status must include version and hash
rules.snippet: Expose pack version, process identity, file hashes, and reload status.

rules.title: MCP explain must match CLI explain
rules.snippet: Keep explain behavior routed through the same registry/doc source as CLI.

rules.title: MCP route must match CLI route
rules.snippet: Keep route behavior routed through the same registry decision tree as CLI.

rules.title: package-lock.json is required
rules.snippet: Commit a package-lock.json and use npm ci so installs are reproducible.

rules.title: Dependency licenses must match policy
rules.snippet: Use approved dependency licenses or add reviewed package policy.

rules.title: Suspicious dependency names are forbidden
rules.snippet: Review typo-squat-like package names before they enter dependencies.

rules.title: SBOM must be generated for release
rules.snippet: Generate SBOM metadata before release or PR-ready claims.

rules.title: Published package files must be explicit
rules.snippet: Set package.json files so releases cannot accidentally publish private harness state.

rules.title: Package bin paths must exist
rules.snippet: Keep every package.json bin target present in the package and runnable by Node.

rules.title: Package export paths must exist
rules.snippet: Keep every package.json exports target pointing at a tracked file or directory.

rules.title: npm ci is required in CI
rules.snippet: Use npm ci in workflow and local parity gates instead of npm install.

rules.title: Enforcer dependencies must be pinned
rules.snippet: Use exact package versions; avoid ^, ~, *, latest, git:, file:, and path ranges.

rules.title: packageManager must pin npm
rules.snippet: Set packageManager to an exact npm version such as npm@11.7.0.

rules.title: Node engine must be bounded
rules.snippet: Use a bounded Node engine range such as >=20 <23 instead of an open-ended range.

rules.title: Dependency install scripts require approval
rules.snippet: Remove dependency install scripts or add reviewed package policy before release.

rules.title: Git dependencies are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use published exact package versions instead of git dependencies.

rules.title: File and path dependencies are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use workspace-approved packages or published exact versions instead of file/path dependencies.

rules.title: npm audit high and critical findings must fail
rules.snippet: Fix high or critical npm audit findings instead of suppressing dependency risk.

rules.title: Platform-specific script commands must be guarded
rules.snippet: Put Windows-only command invocations behind an explicit process.platform guard or use a cross-platform helper.

rules.title: PR-ready claims require fresh proof
rules.snippet: Reject PR-ready proof claims without a fresh matching proof run.

rules.title: Proof registry docs paths must exist
rules.snippet: Keep every proof definition linked to an existing proof/rule doc.

rules.title: Proof capabilities must match environment
rules.snippet: Check declared capabilities before treating proof as executable.

rules.title: Device proof cannot auto-pass on desktop
rules.snippet: Mark Android/iOS/device proof unavailable/manual unless matching capability is present.

rules.title: Proof claims must list proved and unproved claims
rules.snippet: Record claimsProved and claimsNotProved in proof runs and claim output.

rules.title: Proof output must be compact by default
rules.snippet: Return bounded summaries and diagnostics unless artifact text is explicitly requested.

rules.title: Proof exports must redact secrets
rules.snippet: Redact secret-like values from proof artifacts and exported proof manifests.

rules.title: Proof freshness must bind commit and scope
rules.snippet: Store commit, branch, files, and profile with every proof run and claim.

rules.title: Manual-required proof cannot auto-pass
rules.snippet: Represent manual/device proof as manual-required or unavailable unless explicit evidence is attached.

rules.title: Required proof artifacts must exist
rules.snippet: Fail proof claims when required artifacts are missing or empty.

rules.title: Proof artifacts must hash-match
rules.snippet: Compare recorded artifact hashes against claim-time artifacts.

rules.title: Dirty worktrees invalidate PR-ready proof
rules.snippet: Reject PR-ready claims from dirty trees unless allowDirty is explicit.

rules.title: Waived proof must remain visible
rules.snippet: Report waived or unavailable proof as explicit evidence state, not pass.

rules.title: Proof command cannot be empty
rules.snippet: Require a command or classify the proof as manual-required/unavailable.

rules.title: Proof command cannot be a shell string
rules.snippet: Accept command argv arrays; reject shell-string execution for proof commands.

rules.title: Python lint suppression comments are forbidden
rules.snippet: Fix the Ruff/Pylint issue or move the exception into reviewed policy.

rules.title: Python type-ignore comments are forbidden
rules.snippet: Fix the type issue or model the dynamic boundary explicitly.

rules.title: Python naked domain string aliases are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use typing.NewType, a validated dataclass/value object, or a project-owned schema boundary instead of Alias = str.

rules.title: Skipped/focused Python tests are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove skip/focus markers or move the exception into reviewed test policy.

rules.title: Ruff diagnostics must pass
rules.snippet: Run Ruff with JSON output through the Enforcer harness and fix diagnostics.

rules.title: Python type-check diagnostics must pass
rules.snippet: Run Pyright or mypy through the Enforcer harness and fix diagnostics.

rules.title: Python Any is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use precise types, protocols, or validated boundary models instead of Any.

rules.title: Python mutable default arguments are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use None plus explicit construction inside the function body.

rules.title: Broad Python exception handlers are forbidden
rules.snippet: Catch specific exception types and model the failure path.

rules.title: Bare Python except handlers are forbidden
rules.snippet: Catch specific exception types; never use bare except.

rules.title: Python except pass is forbidden
rules.snippet: Handle the exception, return a modeled error, or re-raise with context instead of pass.

rules.title: Python print debugging is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use project logging domains or structured diagnostics instead of print.

rules.title: Python runtime asserts are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use explicit validation and modeled errors instead of assert in production code.

rules.title: Python dynamic code execution is forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove eval, exec, and compile from project source.

rules.title: Python subprocess shell=True is forbidden
rules.snippet: Pass argv arrays with shell disabled and validate every boundary input.

rules.title: Python os.system is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use subprocess with argv arrays and shell disabled instead of os.system.

rules.title: Python pickle.loads is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use safe structured formats and schema decoding instead of untrusted pickle loading.

rules.title: Python functions must be typed
rules.snippet: Add parameter and return annotations so Pyright/mypy can enforce contracts.

rules.title: Python yaml.load requires a safe loader
rules.snippet: Use yaml.safe_load or pass SafeLoader explicitly.

rules.title: Python global mutable state is forbidden
rules.snippet: Move mutable state behind explicit owners or dependency-injected services.

rules.title: Python dynamic imports are forbidden in domain code
rules.snippet: Use static imports at module boundaries instead of importlib or __import__.

rules.title: Python naive datetime calls are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use timezone-aware datetime values instead of datetime.now() or utcnow().

rules.title: Python sleep is forbidden in async code and tests
rules.snippet: Use controlled clocks, awaits on events, or deterministic polling instead of time.sleep.

rules.title: Python HTTP calls must set timeouts
rules.snippet: Pass an explicit timeout to requests calls so tests and services cannot hang.

rules.title: Python asyncio tasks must be tracked
rules.snippet: Store, await, or supervise create_task results instead of fire-and-forget tasks.

rules.title: Python coroutine calls must be awaited or returned
rules.snippet: Await coroutine calls or return them to the caller instead of swallowing them.

rules.title: Python parent-relative imports are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use package-owned absolute imports rather than escaping boundaries with from .. imports.

rules.title: Python wildcard imports are forbidden
rules.snippet: Import explicit names from owning modules instead of using import *.

rules.title: Python return annotations are required
rules.snippet: Add explicit return annotations to every function so contracts are visible to type checkers.

rules.title: Python from-module wildcard imports are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace from module import * with explicit imports.

rules.title: Python dumping-ground module names are forbidden
rules.snippet: Rename utils.py, helpers.py, and common.py to domain-specific modules.

rules.title: Python dataclass value objects must be frozen and slotted
rules.snippet: Use @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) for domain value objects.

rules.title: Python tuple domain records are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use named value objects instead of NamedTuple or tuple positional records.

rules.title: Python raw JSON dict domain inputs are forbidden
rules.snippet: Decode JSON into typed boundary models before entering domain code.

rules.title: Python environment reads must stay in config boundaries
rules.snippet: Read os.environ in config modules and pass typed config inward.

rules.title: Python dict[str, Any] domain APIs are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use precise typed mappings or decoded value objects instead of dict[str, Any].

rules.title: Python raw str ID aliases are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use NewType, frozen dataclasses, or schema-branded values for domain identifiers.

rules.title: Python raw domain parameters are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace ID/count/state raw str/int/bool parameters with typed domain values.

rules.title: TypedDict domain models are forbidden
rules.snippet: Keep TypedDict at boundaries and use value objects or dataclasses for domain models.

rules.title: Pydantic domain models are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use boundary decoders plus domain value objects instead of BaseModel in domain code.

rules.title: Python Optional field soup is forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace clusters of Optional fields with explicit states or value objects.

triggers: pyproject.toml is required for Python projects
rules.title: pyproject.toml is required for Python projects
rules.snippet: Keep Python tool policy in pyproject.toml.

triggers: Python local path dependencies require waiver
rules.title: Python local path dependencies require waiver
rules.snippet: Use published packages or explicit workspace policy instead of path dependencies.

rules.title: Ruff configuration is required
rules.snippet: Add [tool.ruff] configuration to pyproject.toml.

triggers: Pyright or mypy configuration is required
rules.title: Pyright or mypy configuration is required
rules.snippet: Add [tool.pyright] or [tool.mypy] strict type-check configuration.

triggers: Python type checker strict mode is required
rules.title: Python type checker strict mode is required
rules.snippet: Enable strict Pyright or mypy settings.

rules.title: Ruff diagnostics must be structured
rules.snippet: Run Ruff with JSON output through the Enforcer harness.

rules.title: Python type diagnostics must be structured
rules.snippet: Run Pyright with --outputjson or mypy with structured output through the harness.
triggers: Python type diagnostics must be structured

rules.title: Python lockfile is required
rules.snippet: Commit uv.lock, poetry.lock, or the configured Python lockfile.

rules.title: Unpinned Python requirements are forbidden
rules.snippet: Pin requirements with exact versions or hashes.

rules.title: Python git dependencies are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use published pinned packages instead of git dependencies.

rules.title: Python skipped/xfail tests are forbidden without waiver
rules.snippet: Remove skip, skipif, and xfail markers or move an expiring waiver into policy.

rules.title: Python parsers and normalizers require property tests
rules.snippet: Use Hypothesis/given tests for parser and normalizer behavior.
triggers: Python parsers and normalizers require property tests

rules.title: Weak Python assertions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Assert concrete values and modeled outcomes instead of bare truthiness or non-null checks.

rules.title: Empty Python tests are forbidden
rules.snippet: Every test must execute meaningful behavior and assertions.

rules.title: Python tests must assert behavior
rules.snippet: Add concrete assertions to tests instead of only exercising code.

rules.title: Python monkeypatch and mocks are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use real local collaborators or reviewed test seams instead of monkeypatch and mock.

rules.title: Python unit tests must not use the network
rules.snippet: Keep unit tests local and deterministic; move network checks to explicit integration proof.

rules.title: Python sleep-based tests are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use deterministic clocks, events, or bounded polling helpers instead of sleep.

rules.title: Python validators require invalid-input tests
rules.snippet: Add negative tests for validator/parser rejection paths.

rules.title: Python exception paths require tests
rules.snippet: Assert raised exceptions with pytest.raises instead of leaving exception paths unproved.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS is required
rules.snippet: Add CODEOWNERS so policy-critical files require accountable review.

rules.title: License file is required
rules.snippet: Add a root LICENSE file so consumers know the legal terms.

rules.title: Security policy is required
rules.snippet: Add SECURITY.md with vulnerability reporting and supported-version policy.

rules.title: Contributing guide must explain rule changes
rules.snippet: Document how rules, validators, fixtures, schemas, and docs change together.

rules.title: Changelog is required for rule behavior changes
rules.snippet: Keep CHANGELOG.md so validator behavior changes are visible to consumers.

rules.title: Release policy must be documented
rules.snippet: Document release tagging, signing, and package publication policy.

rules.title: Generated schema artifacts must not drift
rules.snippet: Regenerate schema artifacts and keep the working tree clean after schema tests.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS must protect rules
rules.snippet: Add a CODEOWNERS entry for rules/**.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS must protect enforcement source
rules.snippet: Protect scripts/**, src/**, and mcp/**.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS must protect schemas and adapters
rules.snippet: Protect schemas/**, profiles/**, and adapters/**.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS must protect workflows
rules.snippet: Protect .github/workflows/**.

rules.title: Package lockfile is required
rules.snippet: Commit the lockfile used by CI.

rules.title: packageManager is required
rules.snippet: Set packageManager to an exact package-manager version.

rules.title: Node version policy must be bounded
rules.snippet: Use a bounded Node engine policy.

rules.title: Dependency versions must be deterministic
rules.snippet: Use exact dependency versions in package manifests.

rules.title: Rust toolchain must be pinned
rules.snippet: Add rust-toolchain.toml with channel and rustfmt/clippy components.

rules.title: Cargo.lock must exist for deterministic builds
rules.snippet: Run cargo generate-lockfile and commit Cargo.lock unless this is a publish-only library with a documented exception.

rules.title: Clippy configuration must exist
rules.snippet: Add clippy.toml to centralize lint thresholds and future policy knobs.

rules.title: Dependency policy must exist
rules.snippet: Add deny.toml and run cargo deny check in CI.

rules.title: Cargo.toml must declare rust-version
rules.snippet: Set package.rust-version or workspace.package.rust-version.

rules.title: cargo fmt must pass
rules.snippet: Run cargo fmt --all -- --check or the scoped package equivalent before completing the task.

rules.title: cargo clippy hard mode must pass
rules.snippet: Run cargo clippy with -D warnings and selected deny lints.

rules.title: cargo test must pass
rules.snippet: Run cargo test for the workspace or touched crate.

rules.title: rustdoc warnings must be errors
rules.snippet: Run cargo doc with RUSTDOCFLAGS denying rustdoc warnings for release/readiness gates.

rules.title: cargo-deny must pass
rules.snippet: Run cargo deny check to enforce advisories, bans, licenses, and source policy.

rules.title: cargo-deny must be installed when dependency policy is required
rules.snippet: Install with cargo install --locked cargo-deny or disable only in local scan mode.

rules.title: cargo-audit must pass when enabled
rules.snippet: Run cargo audit and upgrade vulnerable crates instead of ignoring advisories.

rules.title: Validated constructors require invalid-input tests
rules.snippet: Add invalid-input tests for each try_new constructor.

rules.title: Parsers require negative and edge-case tests
rules.snippet: Add invalid, empty, oversized, and malformed parser tests.

rules.title: DTO conversions require negative tests
rules.snippet: Add negative tests for boundary DTO to domain conversions.

rules.title: Bugfixes require regression tests
rules.snippet: Add a REGRESSION-TEST marker for bugfix behavior.

rules.title: Domain tests cannot use should_panic casually
rules.snippet: Use Result/assertions instead of #[should_panic] unless testing a panic contract.

rules.title: Tests cannot unwrap without justification
rules.snippet: Use exact assertions or add TEST-UNWRAP-JUSTIFICATION for exceptional unwraps.

rules.title: No weak is_ok assertions
rules.snippet: Assert exact Ok values or error variants instead of only checking is_ok().

rules.title: No weak is_some assertions
rules.snippet: Assert exact Some values instead of only checking is_some().

rules.title: Test bodies cannot be empty
rules.snippet: Add assertions or remove placeholder tests.

rules.title: Construction-only tests must assert behavior
rules.snippet: Assert validation/output instead of only constructing a value.

rules.title: Snapshot tests must redact volatile values
rules.snippet: Redact timestamps, UUIDs, random IDs, and secrets in snapshot tests.

rules.title: Normalizers and parsers require property tests
rules.snippet: Add proptest/quickcheck coverage for normalizers and parsers.

rules.title: Binary and network parsers require fuzz targets
rules.snippet: Add fuzz target evidence for binary or network parser inputs.

rules.title: Concurrency code requires cancellation tests
rules.snippet: Add shutdown/cancellation tests for spawn, channel, select, and async loop code.

rules.title: Unsafe modules require Miri proof
rules.snippet: Add MIRI-PROOF evidence for unsafe modules.

rules.title: Domain structs must not derive Deserialize directly
rules.snippet: Deserialize into DTOs at the boundary, then validate into domain types.

rules.title: Serialize derives require serialization docs
rules.snippet: Document serialization contract before deriving Serialize on domain types.

rules.title: Untagged serde enums require justification
rules.snippet: Avoid #[serde(untagged)] ambiguity or add SERDE-UNTAGGED-JUSTIFICATION at the boundary.

rules.title: serde(default) requires justification
rules.snippet: Add DEFAULT-JUSTIFICATION for serde(default).

rules.title: DTO structs must live in boundary modules
rules.snippet: Move DTO/request/response/envelope structs under boundary, serde, transport, or adapter modules.

rules.title: DTO names must use boundary suffixes
rules.snippet: Name boundary shapes with Dto, Request, Response, Envelope, or configured suffixes.

rules.title: Domain modules cannot import DTO modules
rules.snippet: Move DTO imports to boundary/adapters and convert into domain types.

rules.title: Boundary DTOs must convert to domain explicitly
rules.snippet: Add TryFrom, From, or a named mapper from DTO shapes into domain types.

rules.title: Public serde enums must be tagged
rules.snippet: Use serde(tag = ...) or add SERDE-TAG-JUSTIFICATION.

rules.title: External schema shapes require round-trip tests
rules.snippet: Add round-trip tests for externally serialized shapes.

rules.title: Strict config inputs must deny unknown fields
rules.snippet: Add #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)] to strict config/input DTOs.

rules.title: serde(flatten) requires justification
rules.snippet: Add FLATTEN-JUSTIFICATION for serde(flatten).

rules.title: Domain code cannot use raw base64 strings
rules.snippet: Use branded binary/blob/base64 value objects in domain code.

rules.title: serde_json::from_str is boundary-only
rules.snippet: Decode JSON at boundary modules and pass typed domain values inward.

rules.title: Do not deserialize directly into domain types
rules.snippet: Deserialize into DTOs, then validate into domain types.

rules.title: Runtime Rust source must not contain inline string literals
rules.snippet: Move stable runtime strings to constants, schemas, protocol crates, or configured owner modules.

rules.title: No lint suppression attributes
rules.snippet: Do not use #[allow(...)] or #[expect(...)]. Fix the code or strengthen the rule instead of suppressing it.

rules.title: No validator suppression comments
rules.snippet: Comments such as rust-rules: ignore/allow/skip/disable are forbidden.

rules.title: Unsafe Rust is forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use safe Rust. If unsafe is truly required, isolate it in an approved FFI/sys crate and set allowUnsafeCode with owner review.

rules.title: transmute is forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace transmute with typed conversion, checked parsing, or an isolated reviewed boundary.

rules.title: MaybeUninit is forbidden outside unsafe owners
rules.snippet: Use initialized safe types unless an approved unsafe-owner module carries the invariants.

rules.title: ManuallyDrop requires unsafe justification
rules.snippet: Avoid ManuallyDrop or isolate it behind reviewed unsafe invariants.

rules.title: mem::forget is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use explicit ownership transfer or drop behavior instead of leaking values with mem::forget.

rules.title: Unsafe code requires SAFETY documentation
rules.snippet: Every unsafe block requires a nearby // SAFETY: comment explaining the invariants.

rules.title: Box::leak requires justification
rules.snippet: Avoid permanent leaks or add LEAK-JUSTIFICATION for reviewed singleton state.

rules.title: static mut is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use OnceLock, LazyLock, atomics, or synchronized ownership instead of static mut.

rules.title: UnsafeCell is forbidden outside approved primitives
rules.snippet: Use safe synchronization or isolate UnsafeCell in reviewed concurrency primitives.

rules.title: unsafe Send/Sync impls require safety proof
rules.snippet: Add SAFETY documentation and concurrency tests before unsafe Send or Sync implementations.

rules.title: get_unchecked is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use checked indexing or typed bounds proofs instead of get_unchecked.

rules.title: Raw pointer dereference requires safety proof
rules.snippet: Keep raw pointer dereference inside approved unsafe modules with local SAFETY comments.

rules.title: FFI extern blocks must live in FFI owner modules
rules.snippet: Move extern blocks to ffi/sys modules and wrap them in safe domain APIs.

rules.title: FFI structs require repr(C)
rules.snippet: Annotate FFI-facing structs with #[repr(C)] and keep them in FFI owner modules.

rules.title: no_mangle is restricted to FFI owner modules
rules.snippet: Use #[no_mangle] only in configured FFI export modules.

rules.title: FFI exports must not unwind
rules.snippet: Wrap FFI exports in catch_unwind or use an abort-on-panic profile.

rules.title: Unsafe functions require a # Safety section
rules.snippet: Document caller obligations under a rustdoc # Safety section.

rules.title: Unsafe crates require Miri proof
rules.snippet: Add MIRI-PROOF evidence for unsafe code or remove unsafe constructs.

rules.title: Unsafe crates require cargo-geiger report
rules.snippet: Add GEIGER-PROOF evidence for unsafe code or remove unsafe constructs.

rules.title: Unsafe code is forbidden in tests as an escape hatch
rules.snippet: Tests must prove safe APIs, not bypass invariants with unsafe code.

rules.title: allow(unsafe_code) is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use an approved unsafe profile instead of suppressing unsafe policy with attributes.

rules.title: Raw pointers are forbidden in public/domain APIs
rules.snippet: Use references, NonNull wrappers, or isolated FFI boundary types.

rules.title: No unwrap/expect
rules.snippet: Replace .unwrap()/.expect() with ?, match, ok_or_else, or a typed error.

rules.title: No formatted string errors
rules.snippet: Put dynamic context into typed error fields instead of Err(format!(...)).

rules.title: No map_err to_string error erasure
rules.snippet: Preserve source errors with #[from], #[source], or typed error variants.

rules.title: Boolean success APIs are forbidden
rules.snippet: Return Result or a modeled status enum instead of bool from fallible domain operations.

rules.title: Sentinel error values are forbidden
rules.snippet: Return Option or Result instead of -1, empty string, or other sentinel failure values.

rules.title: Fallible constructors must return Result
rules.snippet: Use try_new/parse returning Result<Self, Error> for raw constructor inputs.

rules.title: main must not swallow fallible errors
rules.snippet: Return Result from main or handle every fallible call explicitly.

rules.title: No ignored fallible results
rules.snippet: Handle the Result, propagate it with ?, or use a typed helper that documents intentional discard.

rules.title: No ok() error swallowing
rules.snippet: Do not turn Result into Option with .ok(); preserve or map the typed error.

rules.title: No unwrap_or_default on fallible domain data
rules.snippet: Handle failure explicitly instead of defaulting away parse/config/domain errors.

rules.title: No unwrap_or hiding parse/config failures
rules.snippet: Handle parse, env, and config failures explicitly instead of replacing them with fallback values.

rules.title: No panic-like macros in production paths
rules.snippet: Replace panic!/todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable! with Result, typed errors, or explicit state modelling.

rules.title: Error enums must implement Debug and Error
rules.snippet: Derive Debug and thiserror::Error or implement std::error::Error for custom error enums.

rules.title: Wrapped source errors require source/from metadata
rules.snippet: Mark wrapped error sources with #[source] or #[from] so diagnostics keep provenance.

rules.title: Do not log and return the same error
rules.snippet: Log at the boundary or return the error, not both in the same function.

rules.title: No debug/console macros in Rust logic
rules.snippet: Use tracing/logging abstractions. dbg!, println!, and eprintln! are not allowed in source logic.

rules.title: Domain code must not use erased application errors
rules.snippet: Use a typed error enum. anyhow::Result and Box<dyn Error> belong only at application boundaries.

rules.title: No stringly Result errors
rules.snippet: Return a typed error enum instead of Result<T, String>.

rules.title: No static string Result errors
rules.snippet: Return a typed error enum instead of Result<T, &'static str>.

rules.title: No literal Err values
rules.snippet: Return a typed error variant instead of Err("...").

rules.title: Clone must be justified
rules.snippet: Add a nearby CLONE-JUSTIFICATION: comment or refactor to borrow, move, Arc, or Cow.

rules.title: String allocation must be justified
rules.snippet: Add ALLOC-JUSTIFICATION: near to_string()/to_owned() or avoid allocation with borrowing/domain types.

rules.title: Unchecked indexing is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use get(), get_mut(), split_at_checked-style logic, or typed indexes that prove bounds.

rules.title: Lossy casts require justification
rules.snippet: Use TryFrom/TryInto or add CAST-JUSTIFICATION: explaining why the cast is safe.

rules.title: No raw string types in domain function signatures
rules.snippet: Replace String/&str/str/Cow<str>/PathBuf/OsString with branded domain types. Raw text may exist only in configured boundary or owner modules.

rules.title: No unbranded primitive types in domain function signatures
rules.snippet: Replace bool/numeric primitive parameters and returns with newtypes, enums, NonZero types, or domain value objects.

rules.title: Serialized domain fields must not expose raw identity primitives
rules.snippet: Use typed domain newtypes or enums for public serialized id/ref/event/command fields.

rules.title: No AsRef<str> domain parameters
rules.snippet: Convert raw text at adapters and accept branded domain types in domain APIs.

rules.title: No Into<String> domain parameters
rules.snippet: Decode into a branded type before entering the domain instead of accepting Into<String>.

rules.title: No Display-based domain identity parameters
rules.snippet: Accept branded identity types instead of impl Display for ID-like domain parameters.

rules.title: No public raw fields
rules.snippet: Public fields must expose branded/domain types, not String, &str, bool, numbers, Vec<String>, or HashMap<String, _>.

rules.title: No Cow<str> in domain APIs
rules.snippet: Normalize raw text at boundaries and pass branded values through domain APIs.

rules.title: No Vec<String> domain APIs
rules.snippet: Use a typed collection of branded values instead of Vec<String>.

rules.title: No HashMap<String, _> domain APIs
rules.snippet: Use a typed key newtype or domain map instead of HashMap<String, _>.

rules.title: No BTreeMap<String, _> domain APIs
rules.snippet: Use a typed key newtype or domain map instead of BTreeMap<String, _>.

rules.title: serde_json::Value is forbidden in domain code
rules.snippet: Decode untyped JSON at the boundary and pass modeled domain values inward.

rules.title: Option<String> is forbidden in domain structs
rules.snippet: Use a domain-specific optional value object instead of optional raw text.

rules.title: Option<bool> is forbidden for domain state
rules.snippet: Use an enum for tri-state or unknown state instead of Option<bool>.

rules.title: No boolean state clusters
rules.snippet: Replace multiple boolean state fields with an enum, typestate, or explicit state value object.

rules.title: Raw Duration is forbidden for named domain timing values
rules.snippet: Use branded timeout, TTL, delay, or deadline value objects instead of raw Duration.

rules.title: Raw time types are forbidden in public domain APIs
rules.snippet: Use branded timestamps or clock-owned value objects instead of SystemTime or Instant.

rules.title: Raw private fields require brand invariants
rules.snippet: Private raw fields inside domain types require a nearby BRAND-INVARIANT: comment explaining validation and meaning.

rules.title: Raw URL strings are forbidden
rules.snippet: Decode URL/URI text into branded URL value objects at boundaries.

rules.title: Raw path strings are forbidden
rules.snippet: Decode file, dir, and path text into branded path value objects at boundaries.

rules.title: ID-like raw fields are forbidden
rules.snippet: Represent id/ref/key fields with branded newtypes instead of raw strings or integers.

rules.title: Public newtype fields are forbidden
rules.snippet: Keep tuple newtype fields private and expose validated constructors/accessors.

rules.title: Newtypes must have validated constructors
rules.snippet: Add try_new or parse constructors for raw-field newtypes.

rules.title: Newtype constructors must not panic
rules.snippet: Return Result from constructors instead of panicking or unwrapping validation.

rules.title: Numeric ID newtypes should use NonZero types
rules.snippet: Use NonZero numeric wrappers or validated domain IDs instead of raw numeric ID fields.

rules.title: No type aliases to domain collections
rules.snippet: Use collection newtypes instead of type aliases to Vec/HashMap/BTreeMap.

rules.title: Naked tuples are forbidden in public signatures
rules.snippet: Return named structs or domain value objects instead of positional tuples.

rules.title: Multi-primitive constructors are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use a named input value object for constructors with multiple primitive parameters.

rules.title: Type aliases must not disguise raw primitives
rules.snippet: Use tuple/newtype structs instead of type UserId = String or type Count = usize.

rules.title: Domain value objects must implement Debug intentionally
rules.snippet: Derive or implement Debug for public domain value objects, unless they are secret types.

rules.title: Secret types must not derive Debug or Display
rules.snippet: Implement redacted formatting for secret, token, key, and credential types.

rules.title: Secret types must have redacted formatting
rules.snippet: Implement redacted Debug/Display for secret, token, key, credential, and password types.

rules.title: Tuple newtypes must not expose raw inner fields
rules.snippet: Use pub struct UserId(String); not pub struct UserId(pub String); and validate construction.

rules.title: Wildcard imports are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace use x::* with explicit imports.

rules.title: Wildcard public re-exports are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace pub use x::* with direct module imports or explicit call-site paths.

rules.title: Public re-exports must match project policy
rules.snippet: For strict projects, remove pub use entirely. For facade projects, keep pub use only in configured facade files.

rules.title: Dumping-ground modules are forbidden
rules.snippet: Rename utils/helpers/common/misc/shared/stuff to a domain-specific module.

rules.title: Build scripts are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Remove build.rs or explicitly approve it in config with a deterministic justification.

rules.title: Blocking primitives are forbidden inside async modules
rules.snippet: Do not combine async/.await with std::sync locks, std::thread::sleep, or blocking std::fs/std::net calls.

rules.title: std sync locks are forbidden in async modules
rules.snippet: Use async-aware synchronization or isolate blocking state outside async execution paths.

rules.title: Do not await while holding lock guards
rules.snippet: Drop lock guards before await or use async-aware locking patterns.

rules.title: tokio::spawn handles must be tracked
rules.snippet: Store, await, or supervise JoinHandle values instead of fire-and-forget spawning.

rules.title: Fire-and-forget spawn requires task justification
rules.snippet: Store the JoinHandle or add TASK-JUSTIFICATION explaining supervision and shutdown.

rules.title: C-style index loops are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use iterators, enumerate(), chunks(), windows(), or typed ranges.

rules.title: Unbounded channels require justification
rules.snippet: Use bounded channels or add CHANNEL-JUSTIFICATION explaining the backpressure boundary.

rules.title: External async I/O must use timeouts
rules.snippet: Wrap network and service futures in a timeout policy instead of awaiting indefinitely.

rules.title: Retry loops require bounded policy
rules.snippet: Use a RetryPolicy or bounded backoff object instead of ad-hoc retry loops.

rules.title: Async loops require cancellation
rules.snippet: Add a cancellation branch or shutdown signal to async loop constructs.

rules.title: select! branches require cancellation-safety notes
rules.snippet: Add CANCEL-SAFE notes explaining cancellation behavior for select! branches.

rules.title: Blocking I/O is forbidden in async modules
rules.snippet: Use async file/network APIs or spawn_blocking for unavoidable blocking work.

rules.title: CPU work in async paths must be isolated
rules.snippet: Move CPU-heavy work to spawn_blocking or a worker boundary.

rules.title: Libraries must not create global Tokio runtimes
rules.snippet: Accept a runtime from the application boundary instead of constructing one in a library.

rules.title: block_on is forbidden in library/domain code
rules.snippet: Keep async execution at application boundaries and expose async APIs inward.

rules.title: Sleep-based tests are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use controlled clocks, notifications, or deterministic polling instead of sleeping in tests.

rules.title: Arc<Mutex<T>> signatures must be domain-named
rules.snippet: Wrap shared mutable state in a named domain type instead of exposing raw Arc<Mutex<T>>.

rules.title: Dependency versions must be pinned semver requirements
rules.snippet: Do not use dependency version = "*".

rules.title: Loose dependency version ranges are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use pinned semver requirements instead of *, >=, or major-only dependency ranges.

rules.title: Heavy dependencies need explicit default feature policy
rules.snippet: Set default-features explicitly for heavy runtime crates.

rules.title: New dependencies require justification
rules.snippet: Add DEPENDENCY-JUSTIFICATION near new direct dependencies or in policy.

rules.title: Duplicate direct dependency versions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Align direct dependency requirements across workspace members.

rules.title: Git dependencies are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use crates.io versions or explicitly approve a pinned git revision in dependency policy.

rules.title: Runtime proc-macro dependencies require approval
rules.snippet: Move proc-macro crates to build/dev dependencies or add approval.

rules.title: Native dependencies require approval
rules.snippet: Add NATIVE-DEPENDENCY-JUSTIFICATION for native/sys/build-linked crates.

rules.title: GPL and AGPL licenses are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use an approved license or reviewed dependency policy before introducing copyleft dependencies.

rules.title: Yanked crate versions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Configure dependency policy to deny yanked versions.

rules.title: Unmaintained crates are forbidden when advisory data exists
rules.snippet: Configure advisory policy to deny or explicitly review unmaintained crates.

rules.title: Cargo.lock must be current after manifest changes
rules.snippet: Run cargo generate-lockfile or cargo update after changing Cargo.toml.

rules.title: Workspace dependencies must use path/workspace linkage
rules.snippet: Use workspace/path dependencies between workspace members, not registry versions.

rules.title: Dev dependencies must not leak into production
rules.snippet: Move test-only crates to dev-dependencies.

rules.title: Test-only crates are restricted to dev-dependencies
rules.snippet: Use dev-dependencies for test-only crates such as proptest, rstest, mockall, and criterion.

rules.title: Runtime crates cannot depend on test-only crates
rules.snippet: Remove test-only crates from runtime dependencies.

rules.title: Path dependencies are forbidden outside policy
rules.snippet: Use workspace members or explicitly approve path dependencies.

rules.title: build-dependencies require approval
rules.snippet: Avoid build-time code execution or add a reviewed build dependency waiver.

rules.title: Workspace path dependencies must stay inside the workspace
rules.snippet: Move path dependencies under the workspace root or publish/version the dependency.

rules.title: Direct dependency versions must not drift
rules.snippet: Align direct registry dependency requirements across changed manifests.

rules.title: SBOM generation must complete
rules.snippet: Generate package metadata artifacts without committing generated output to source.

rules.title: Scanner must mask string literals where appropriate
rules.snippet: Mask strings before scanning patterns that should only match code structure.

rules.title: Scanner output must be sorted
rules.snippet: Sort findings by normalized path, line, rule ID, and message.

rules.title: Scanner must bound file reads
rules.snippet: Use size limits or streaming before scanning arbitrary files.

rules.title: Scanner must skip binary files safely
rules.snippet: Detect binary payloads and avoid UTF-8 parser crashes.

rules.title: Invalid UTF-8 must produce bounded diagnostics
rules.snippet: Handle decode failures with a compact diagnostic instead of crashing.

rules.title: Unknown extensions must not trigger false language scans
rules.snippet: Route files by configured language extensions and return no detailed docs for unknown extensions.

rules.title: Diff scope must handle deleted and renamed files
rules.snippet: Resolve diff entries deterministically and skip deleted files safely.

rules.title: File scope must not scan the whole repo
rules.snippet: Limit file-scope checks to the provided paths.

rules.title: Workspace scope must scan configured roots
rules.snippet: Use configured roots and ignore globs for workspace scans.

rules.title: Crate/package scope must resolve manifests
rules.snippet: Resolve Cargo/package manifests before scoped native checks.

rules.title: Scope reports must include included/excluded counts
rules.snippet: Report scope file counts and ignored/excluded paths in compact output.

rules.title: Scanner must mask comments where appropriate
rules.snippet: Mask comments before structural scans while keeping suppression-comment checks explicit.

rules.title: Doctor must expose ignore globs
rules.snippet: Show ignore directories and file globs in doctor output.

rules.title: Scanner must still detect suppression comments
rules.snippet: Keep lint-disable, noqa, ts-ignore, and type-ignore detection active.

rules.title: Scanner must handle CRLF and LF identically
rules.snippet: Split and count lines with CRLF/LF-safe logic.

rules.title: Scanner must support Unicode paths
rules.snippet: Normalize paths without assuming ASCII-only file names.

rules.title: Scanner must support spaces in paths
rules.snippet: Treat file paths as data, not shell fragments.

rules.title: Scanner must support Windows drive paths
rules.snippet: Resolve absolute Windows paths correctly before normalizing reports.

rules.title: Scanner symlink policy must be explicit
rules.snippet: Skip or classify symlinks intentionally instead of following them accidentally.

rules.title: Scanner must avoid symlink loops
rules.snippet: Use lstat/realpath guards and never recurse indefinitely through symlinks.

rules.title: Rust strict mode must use cargo metadata
rules.snippet: Use cargo metadata for workspace/package dependency context.

rules.title: Native and regex reports must merge without duplicate spam
rules.snippet: Dedupe merged diagnostics by tool, rule, file, line, and message.

rules.title: Rust strict mode must use parser-backed checks
rules.snippet: Use parser/native checks for signatures, fields, and syntax-sensitive Rust rules where regex is insufficient.

rules.title: Rust strict mode must ingest Clippy JSON
rules.snippet: Normalize clippy JSON diagnostics through the harness.

rules.title: Rust strict mode must ingest rustdoc warnings
rules.snippet: Normalize rustdoc JSON or warning output through the harness.

rules.title: TypeScript strict mode must use compiler or ESLint JSON
rules.snippet: Ingest tsc and ESLint diagnostics instead of relying only on regex preflight.

rules.title: Python strict mode must ingest Ruff JSON
rules.snippet: Normalize Ruff JSON diagnostics through the harness.

rules.title: Python strict mode must ingest Pyright or mypy output
rules.snippet: Normalize Pyright/mypy diagnostics through the harness.

rules.title: Security strict mode must ingest SARIF
rules.snippet: Normalize SARIF results from security tools.

rules.title: Regex scanner must remain fast preflight
rules.snippet: Keep regex scanners as bounded preflight before heavier native tools.

rules.title: Inline secrets are forbidden
rules.snippet: Move credentials into a secret manager or local ignored environment file.

rules.title: Sensitive files are forbidden in source scope
rules.snippet: Do not commit .env files, private keys, mobile service secrets, or credential bundles.

rules.title: GitHub tokens are forbidden
rules.snippet: Move GitHub tokens to a secret manager and keep only placeholders in examples.

rules.title: High-entropy secret assignments are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace real-looking high-entropy values with obvious placeholders.

rules.title: .env.example may contain placeholders only
rules.snippet: Use placeholder values such as example, changeme, or <TOKEN>.

rules.title: .env.template may contain placeholders only
rules.snippet: Do not put real-looking secrets in template environment files.

rules.title: Secrets are forbidden in snapshots
rules.snippet: Redact snapshots before committing them.

rules.title: Fixture secrets require fake markers
rules.snippet: Mark fixture credentials as fake and keep them non-secret-looking.

triggers: Secret diagnostics must redact matched values
rules.title: Secret diagnostics must redact matched values
rules.snippet: Report secret classes and locations, never raw secret values.

rules.title: Secret scanners must emit SARIF
rules.snippet: Configure secret scanners to emit SARIF or structured output before CI consumes findings.

rules.title: Gitleaks findings must be normalized
rules.snippet: Run Gitleaks through Enforcer or emit SARIF so findings map to SEC rule IDs.

rules.title: TruffleHog findings must be normalized
rules.snippet: Run TruffleHog through Enforcer or emit JSON so findings map to SEC rule IDs.

rules.title: Committed SSH keys are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove SSH key files and rotate exposed keys.

rules.title: AWS access keys are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove AWS keys from source and rotate exposed credentials.

triggers: Mobile secret config files are forbidden
rules.title: Mobile secret config files are forbidden
rules.snippet: Do not commit GoogleService-Info.plist or google-services.json.

triggers: Google service account JSON is forbidden
rules.title: Google service account JSON is forbidden
rules.snippet: Keep service account JSON in secret storage, not source or fixtures.

rules.title: Azure credentials are forbidden
rules.snippet: Move Azure tenant/client secrets to managed secret storage.

triggers: Slack and Discord tokens are forbidden
rules.title: Slack and Discord tokens are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove chat tokens and use local ignored environment values.

rules.title: JWT-looking secrets are forbidden
rules.snippet: Do not commit JWT tokens or bearer credentials.

rules.title: Private key blocks are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove private keys from source and rotate the exposed key material.

rules.title: Package registry tokens are forbidden
rules.snippet: Keep npm, PyPI, and Cargo tokens in CI secret storage.

rules.title: Stripe keys are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove Stripe keys from source and rotate exposed values.

rules.title: Source files must stay within shape limits
rules.snippet: Split oversized files, long functions, and dumping-ground modules before adding behavior.

rules.title: Placeholder implementation markers are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace TODO, placeholder, not-implemented, debug-print, and temporary code with real behavior before landing.

triggers: File line budget must be respected
rules.title: File line budget must be respected
rules.snippet: Split oversized files before adding behavior.

rules.title: Placeholder implementation markers are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove TODO, FIXME, placeholder, scaffold, and not-implemented code.

rules.title: Copied huge source blocks are forbidden
rules.snippet: Extract shared logic or generate code instead of pasting large copied blocks.
triggers: Copied huge source blocks are forbidden

rules.title: Duplicate function names in one module are forbidden
rules.snippet: Merge duplicate functions or give each behavior one owned name.
triggers: Duplicate function names in one module are forbidden

rules.title: Mixed responsibility source files are forbidden
rules.snippet: Split files that import UI, domain, data, network, and infrastructure layers together.
triggers: Mixed responsibility source files are forbidden

rules.title: Internal modules cannot expose public API
rules.snippet: Keep internal modules private and expose reviewed facades from public boundaries.
triggers: Internal modules cannot expose public API

rules.title: Dependency direction violations are forbidden
rules.snippet: Keep lower-level domain modules independent from app, UI, adapter, and infra layers.

triggers: Function line budget must be respected
rules.title: Function line budget must be respected
rules.snippet: Split long functions into smaller owned operations.

triggers: Export count budget must be respected
rules.title: Export count budget must be respected
rules.snippet: Reduce public surface area or split modules by ownership.

triggers: Type count budget must be respected
rules.title: Type count budget must be respected
rules.snippet: Split type-heavy modules by domain concept.

triggers: Class/struct count budget must be respected
rules.title: Class/struct count budget must be respected
rules.snippet: Keep class and struct ownership focused.

triggers: Nesting depth budget must be respected
rules.title: Nesting depth budget must be respected
rules.snippet: Flatten nested control flow and extract decision objects.

rules.title: Branch budget must be respected
rules.snippet: Reduce conditional complexity or split branching logic.

rules.title: Dumping-ground source filenames are forbidden
rules.snippet: Rename utils, helpers, common, misc, shared, and stuff to domain-specific names.

rules.title: Temporary code comments are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace temporary, for now, hack, and quick fix markers with real implementation.

rules.title: Test doubles are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use real domain contracts, real parsers, and real local services instead of mocks, fakes, stubs, or spies.

rules.title: Weak assertions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Assert concrete behavior and values instead of existence, truthiness, or broad matcher placeholders.

rules.title: Hidden, focused, or ignored tests are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove focused, skipped, todo, ignored, or fixme markers before claiming test coverage.

rules.title: Source workspaces must have test scaffolds
rules.snippet: Add package/crate tests before treating source work as complete.

rules.title: Tests must live in organized test roots
rules.snippet: Move inline unit tests out of source files and into tests/ or another configured test root.

rules.title: TypeScript/JavaScript re-exports are forbidden
rules.snippet: Import from the owning module at the call site; do not add barrel exports or re-export shims.

rules.title: Direct Zod source usage is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use Effect Schema through domain-owned schemas instead of importing or exposing Zod directly.

rules.title: Naked domain string aliases are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use Effect Schema brands plus decode helpers instead of type FooId = string or manual __brand intersections.

rules.title: TypeScript/JavaScript suppression comments are forbidden
rules.snippet: Fix the type/lint issue or add a project policy exception rather than suppressing validation.

rules.title: Skipped/focused JavaScript tests are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove .skip/.only/.todo and keep every checked-in test executable.

rules.title: Import boundary policy must be respected
rules.snippet: Move code to the owning package or add a reviewed import-boundary policy exception instead of crossing layers directly.

rules.title: TypeScript compiler checks must pass
rules.snippet: Run tsc --noEmit through the Enforcer harness and fix compiler diagnostics.

rules.title: ESLint JSON diagnostics must pass
rules.snippet: Run ESLint with --format json through the Enforcer harness and fix lint diagnostics.

rules.title: TypeScript any is forbidden
rules.snippet: Model unknown input with decoders and precise types; do not use any.

rules.title: Record<string, domain> APIs are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use a branded key type or domain map instead of Record<string, T>.

rules.title: Map<string, domain> APIs are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use branded key maps or domain collections instead of Map<string, T>.

triggers: String arrays are forbidden in domain APIs
rules.title: String arrays are forbidden in domain APIs
rules.snippet: Use typed collections or branded values instead of string[] domain surfaces.

rules.title: TypeScript default exports are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use named exports from owning modules and avoid default export ambiguity.

rules.title: Index barrels are forbidden
rules.snippet: Import from owning modules directly; do not use index.ts barrel re-exports.

rules.title: TypeScript namespace declarations are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use modules and explicit imports instead of namespace declarations.

triggers: TypeScript enums are forbidden by default
rules.title: TypeScript enums are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use union literals or configured enum policy instead of unchecked enum surfaces.

triggers: Ambient declare global is forbidden outside type owners
rules.title: Ambient declare global is forbidden outside type owners
rules.snippet: Keep global augmentation in owned type-boundary modules only.

rules.title: process.env is forbidden outside config boundaries
rules.snippet: Read environment variables in a config boundary and pass typed values inward.

rules.title: JSON.parse is forbidden outside decoder boundaries
rules.snippet: Use Effect Schema or a typed decoder for untrusted JSON input.

rules.title: TypeScript unknown cannot escape boundaries
rules.snippet: Decode unknown at the boundary and expose precise domain types inward.

triggers: Date is forbidden in domain APIs
rules.title: Date is forbidden in domain APIs
rules.snippet: Use branded time values or decoded timestamps instead of raw Date domain surfaces.

rules.title: Promise<any> and Promise<unknown> are forbidden
rules.snippet: Return precise promise result types after boundary decoding.

rules.title: Floating promises are forbidden
rules.snippet: Await promises, return them, or intentionally wrap them in a tracked task boundary.

rules.title: Swallowed promise catches are forbidden
rules.snippet: Handle promise failures explicitly; do not use empty catch callbacks.

rules.title: console logging is forbidden in source
rules.snippet: Use project logging domains or structured diagnostics instead of console logging.

rules.title: Throwing string errors is forbidden
rules.snippet: Throw typed Error objects or return modeled domain errors.

triggers: return null is forbidden in domain APIs
rules.title: return null is forbidden in domain APIs
rules.snippet: Use an explicit Option/Result-like type instead of null domain returns.

rules.title: undefined domain state is forbidden
rules.snippet: Model absence explicitly instead of returning or storing undefined as state.

triggers: Optional domain fields are forbidden by default
rules.title: Optional domain fields are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use explicit state unions or value objects instead of partial optional domain objects.

triggers: Partial<T> is forbidden in domain logic
rules.title: Partial<T> is forbidden in domain logic
rules.snippet: Create explicit patch/input types instead of broad Partial<T> domain values.

rules.title: TypeScript type assertions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace as-casts with Effect Schema decoding, narrowing, or typed constructors.

rules.title: Record<string, unknown> payloads are forbidden
rules.snippet: Decode command and event payloads into typed schemas instead of raw records.

triggers: Timer sleeps are forbidden by default
rules.title: Timer sleeps are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use fake clocks, deterministic events, or harness polling instead of setTimeout sleeps.

triggers: Dynamic imports are forbidden in domain code
rules.title: Dynamic imports are forbidden in domain code
rules.snippet: Use static imports and explicit boundaries instead of import().

triggers: child_process is forbidden outside script boundaries
rules.title: child_process is forbidden outside script boundaries
rules.snippet: Move process spawning to reviewed scripts or harness adapters.

rules.title: Dynamic code execution is forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove eval, Function constructors, and dynamic code execution.

triggers: Spreading raw DTOs into domain objects is forbidden
rules.title: Spreading raw DTOs into domain objects is forbidden
rules.snippet: Map decoded fields explicitly instead of spreading raw, dto, payload, or json objects.

triggers: Spreading any into domain objects is forbidden
rules.title: Spreading any into domain objects is forbidden
rules.snippet: Decode and construct domain values explicitly instead of spreading any values.

triggers: Exported functions require explicit return types
rules.title: Exported functions require explicit return types
rules.snippet: Add explicit return types to exported functions so API contracts cannot drift.

triggers: Exported object literals cannot be inferred APIs
rules.title: Exported object literals cannot be inferred APIs
rules.snippet: Annotate exported object APIs or replace them with named typed values.

triggers: Use const instead of single-assignment let
rules.title: Use const instead of single-assignment let
rules.snippet: Use const for local values unless reassignment is required.

rules.title: TypeScript double assertions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace as unknown as casts with real decoding or narrowing.

triggers: Mutating imported or shared objects is forbidden
rules.title: Mutating imported or shared objects is forbidden
rules.snippet: Return new values or use explicit owners instead of mutating imported/shared state.

rules.title: TypeScript non-null assertions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Prove presence with control flow, schema decoding, or explicit error handling.

triggers: TypeScript definite assignment assertions are forbidden
rules.title: TypeScript definite assignment assertions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Initialize fields through constructors or modeled lifecycle states instead of prop! assertions.

rules.title: Raw string domain aliases are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use Effect Schema branded domain types instead of type FooId = string.

triggers: Raw number domain values are forbidden
rules.title: Raw number domain values are forbidden
rules.snippet: Use branded IDs, counts, durations, and units instead of naked number domain parameters.

triggers: Raw boolean domain parameters are forbidden
rules.title: Raw boolean domain parameters are forbidden
rules.snippet: Replace boolean flags with explicit state/value objects or named option types.

rules.title: TypeScript strict mode is required
rules.snippet: Enable strict compiler options instead of relying on partial type checking.

rules.title: Package manager lockfile is required
rules.snippet: Commit a package lockfile for TypeScript packages.

triggers: Loose npm dependency versions are forbidden
rules.title: Loose npm dependency versions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Pin npm dependencies exactly; do not use ^, ~, *, latest, git, or path ranges.

rules.title: npm ci is required in CI
rules.snippet: Use npm ci in workflows and scripts that represent CI gates.
triggers: npm ci is required in CI

rules.title: ESLint must enforce unsafe TypeScript rules
rules.snippet: Enable no-floating-promises, no-explicit-any, and no-unsafe rules in ESLint config.
triggers: ESLint must enforce unsafe TypeScript rules
Zod dependencies are forbidden by policy
rules.title: Zod dependencies are forbidden by policy
rules.snippet: Use Effect Schema through Enforcer policy instead of adding Zod dependencies.

triggers: Duplicate package managers are forbidden by default
rules.title: Duplicate package managers are forbidden by default
