rules.snippet: Use one configured package manager and one lockfile family per project.

rules.title: noImplicitAny is required
rules.snippet: Enable noImplicitAny in tsconfig.

rules.title: strictNullChecks is required
rules.snippet: Enable strictNullChecks in tsconfig.

rules.title: noUncheckedIndexedAccess is required
rules.snippet: Enable noUncheckedIndexedAccess in tsconfig.

rules.title: exactOptionalPropertyTypes is required
rules.snippet: Enable exactOptionalPropertyTypes in tsconfig.

rules.title: noImplicitOverride is required
rules.snippet: Enable noImplicitOverride in tsconfig.

rules.title: noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature is required
rules.snippet: Enable noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature in tsconfig.

rules.title: useUnknownInCatchVariables is required
rules.snippet: Enable useUnknownInCatchVariables in tsconfig.

rules.title: skipLibCheck policy must be explicit
rules.snippet: Set skipLibCheck explicitly so consumers know the project policy.

triggers: Skipped and focused TypeScript tests are forbidden
rules.title: Skipped and focused TypeScript tests are forbidden
rules.snippet: Remove .skip, .only, and .todo from checked-in tests.

rules.title: Decoder and schema tests require negative cases
rules.snippet: Add invalid-input, malformed, or throws/rejects tests for decoders and schemas.
triggers: Decoder and schema tests require negative cases

rules.title: TypeScript expect.any assertions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Assert concrete values instead of expect.anything or expect.any(String/Number).

rules.title: Weak TypeScript assertions are forbidden
rules.snippet: Assert concrete behavior instead of toBeTruthy, toBeDefined, or not.toThrow.

rules.title: Empty TypeScript tests are forbidden
rules.snippet: Every test must exercise behavior and include assertions.

rules.title: TypeScript tests must assert behavior
rules.snippet: Add explicit assertions to every test body.

triggers: Network calls are forbidden in TypeScript unit tests
rules.title: Network calls are forbidden in TypeScript unit tests
rules.snippet: Move network checks to explicit integration proofs.

triggers: Real timers are forbidden in deterministic TypeScript tests
rules.title: Real timers are forbidden in deterministic TypeScript tests
rules.snippet: Use fake clocks, events, or controlled polling instead of real timers.

triggers: TypeScript test doubles are forbidden by default
rules.title: TypeScript test doubles are forbidden by default
rules.snippet: Use real local collaborators and domain contracts instead of mocks, stubs, fakes, or spies.

rules.title: Snapshots cannot contain volatile values
rules.snippet: Redact timestamps, UUIDs, and random IDs before snapshot assertions.

rules.title: Waivers must include required metadata
rules.snippet: Add ruleId, waiverId, owner, issue, reason, exact scope, expires, remediation, and ciAllowed.

rules.title: Waivers require remediation plans
rules.snippet: Explain the concrete cleanup path so waiver debt expires into work, not permanent bypass.

rules.title: Waiver scope must be narrow
rules.snippet: Use exact files or narrow globs; repo-wide and language-wide waivers are forbidden.

rules.title: Expired waivers fail
rules.snippet: Refresh or remove expired waiver debt before claiming the gate passes.

rules.title: Immutable rules cannot be waived unless marked waivable
rules.snippet: Do not add waivers for immutable rules unless the registry explicitly permits waiving that rule.

rules.title: CI waiver behavior must be explicit
rules.snippet: Set ciAllowed intentionally; local-only waivers cannot satisfy CI gates.

rules.title: Waivers must remain visible in output
rules.snippet: Keep waiver IDs attached to waived findings so debt stays visible.
triggers: Waivers must remain visible in output

rules.title: Active waiver count is budgeted
rules.snippet: Reduce active waivers or raise maxActiveWaivers through reviewed policy.

rules.title: Permanent waiver grandfathering is forbidden
rules.snippet: Use short expiry windows for waivers; long-lived waivers need renewed ownership.

rules.title: Waiver owner must be a human or team
rules.snippet: Use a real accountable owner, not codex, ai, agent, or empty metadata.
